response.justification: A strong, versatile engineer with 10+ years across web, mobile, embedded, and high-scale backend systems (notably millions of HTTP requests/sec at Sociomantic), solid craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development), and a relevant CS degree. However, this is a Principal/IC6 role centered on growth and experimentation systems, and the resume shows no experience with experimentation platforms, A/B testing, feature flagging, telemetry/metrics infrastructure, or statistical analysis — the core of the job. There is also limited evidence of principal-level scope (multi-team architecture ownership, cross-org influence). The engineering foundation is good, but the specialization gap is significant.
strengths: High-scale, low-latency distributed systems experience (millions of requests/sec)
Broad multi-language depth (C/C++, C#, Java, JS/TS) plus strong CS fundamentals and CS degree
Demonstrated software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, and developer tooling
concerns: No experimentation, growth, or feature-flagging platform experience (core requirement)
Limited demonstrated data aptitude with metrics, telemetry, and experimental analysis
Title and scope (Senior/Staff IC) below the Principal/IC6 multi-team leadership bar
